message:
chore(beads): sync issue store — close cuqvo, open 0k3d4 postmortem

- cuqvo (REVIEW R2 P3): closed after fcp-e2e legacy compliance adapters
  were migrated off fabricated handshake responses onto real-handshake
  template (cascade of adapter unmasks: docusign/bigquery/gitlab and
  19+ others landed in recent commits).
- 0k3d4 (REVIEW-POSTMORTEM P2): new open bug filed from fresh-eyes
  review of 8c49b234 (br-jdaro). Flags the legacy track() shim in
  cancellation.rs:~90 as a backwards-compat path that violates the
  AGENTS.md "no shims, no tech debt" policy. Scopes a ~60-call-site
  mechanical migration to track_with_owner(id, None) to eliminate
  the silently-cross-cancellable path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0cncv","title":"[HARDENING][MEDIUM] fcp-bootstrap cert validity lacks clock-skew tolerance","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1223 and :1249 both call parsed.validity().is_valid() to check X.509 NotBefore/NotAfter against system time, with no skew tolerance. A machine with clock drift (cold-boot before NTP sync, VM clock, etc.) can silently reject freshly-provisioned certificates whose NotBefore is seconds in the future from the host's perspective, producing a cryptic NoVerifiedIssuerChain error with no hint that it's a clock issue.

Standard PKI practice is to accept certs within a small skew window (±60s typical).

Fix: add a helper cert_valid_with_skew(&parsed, 60_secs) that uses parsed.validity().not_before / .not_after and OffsetDateTime::now_utc() directly, accepting a forward-skew on NotBefore. Keep NotAfter strict (never accept expired). Apply at both call sites.

Not a vulnerability — defensive hardening for cold-bootstrap robustness on clock-skewed machines.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:58:52.658144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370550Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:27:38.370246Z","close_reason":"Completed: added 60s NotBefore bootstrap skew tolerance while keeping NotAfter strict, with certificate-selection regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","title":"[fcp-e2e] Enable kill_on_drop for connector subprocess runner","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs claimed to avoid zombie leaks in Drop, but it only called Child::start_kill(). The underlying asupersync Child docs state start_kill/kill do not wait and still require wait() to reap. Switch the spawned command to kill_on_drop(true), remove the misleading manual Drop path, and add regression coverage that a dropped long-running runner no longer leaves its PID present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:44:41.568206Z","created_by":"ChartreuseCardinal","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422700Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:33.422177Z","close_reason":"Stale-complete: kill_on_drop implementation and regression coverage already present in worktree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1993,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0iek1","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Verified current worktree state for this bead.

Findings:
- `crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` already sets `Command::kill_on_drop(true)` in `ConnectorProcessRunner::spawn()`.
- The old manual-Drop approach is no longer present.
- A Unix regression test exists at `drop_reaps_long_running_process`, which checks that dropping a long-running runner makes its PID disappear.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-e2e --all-targets` passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt -p fcp-e2e -- --check` passed.
- `ubs crates/fcp-e2e/src/subprocess.rs` is not trustworthy for this single Rust file in this repo state; UBS misclassified the path across multiple language scanners and emitted obvious false positives like JavaScript loose-equality warnings on Rust syntax.
- A targeted `rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-e2e --lib drop_reaps_long_running_process -- --nocapture` did not reach the `fcp-e2e` test body because the remote build hit an unrelated compile blocker in `crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/hpke.rs` on the worker, despite the local worktree already containing a fix for that line.
- Earlier remote test runs also hit separate `rch` artifact-retrieval failures caused by pathological nested paths under `.beads/recovery_*`; that is operational fallout, not evidence against this bead implementation.

Conclusion: the requested `kill_on_drop` change and regression coverage are already present in the current worktree, so this bead is stale-complete from a backlog perspective. Remaining noise is in unrelated verification infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-03-25T19:32:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","title":"[FCP3/P4.3] Unify runtime state, error mapping, and health and checkpoint adapters","description":"Background: even with shared kernel and SDK contracts, runtime state and failure handling can drift if error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapters are implemented separately by host, SDK, and connector families.

Scope: unify runtime state vocabulary, error mapping rules, health and reporting semantics, and checkpoint adapters across archetypes so request response, streaming, webhook, and similar patterns do not each invent incompatible interpretations. Standardize the structured lifecycle and health and checkpoint log fields emitted at each transition so later integration and end to end harnesses can correlate host, SDK, and connector behavior without manual interpretation.

Acceptance: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations, and the same vocabulary powers diagnostics and regression tests instead of spawning parallel interpretations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.439780Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42.148877Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:50.136219Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","health","mesh-first","phase-4","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.589697Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:33.073860Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2213,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Claiming after closing blocker tbjdk (P4.1). Starting with code archaeology: mapping runtime state vocabulary across fcp-host, fcp-sdk, fcp-kernel, and connector archetypes to identify divergent error mappings, health semantics, and checkpoint adapter patterns. Goal: one vocabulary for runtime behavior that powers diagnostics and tests across all execution forms.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:52Z"},{"id":2217,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow 0jm9a follow-on in crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs only. Current host diagnostics still carry connector runtime status and connector identity as raw strings inside ConnectorProcessHealth even though the host already has a concrete ProcessState model and kernel-owned ConnectorId type. Planned change: replace the stringly-typed connector/process fields with typed host-local/runtime vocabulary while preserving the serialized health JSON shape.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:53:05Z"},{"id":2219,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a narrow health.rs follow-on. ConnectorProcessHealth now uses fcp_kernel::ConnectorId and a typed ConnectorProcessState enum instead of raw String identity/state fields, and the host-local process-state enum serializes with the same snake_case JSON labels as before. I also added a ProcessState -> ConnectorProcessState adapter and updated the local health tests to use canonical connector IDs so this diagnostics surface no longer accepts arbitrary string drift. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on crates/fcp-host/src/health.rs. Remote verification used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host; exact sync completed successfully, remote dependency preflight verified 26 roots on worker vmi1149989, remote cargo then failed because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed on that worker, and I interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:58:04Z"},{"id":2222,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Added canonical HTTP→FCP error mapping in fcp-sdk/src/migration.rs: map_http_status(status, service, message) provides single source of truth for HTTP-to-FCP error taxonomy. Maps 400→InvalidRequest(1001), 401→Unauthorized(2001), 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 408→UpstreamTimeout, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External(retryable for 500/502/503/504). Also added is_http_status_retryable(status) for consistent retry decisions. 11 new tests, 955 SDK tests pass via rch. Connectors should adopt map_http_status instead of ad-hoc mappings. Next: address Slack ConnectorErrorMapping gap and health vocabulary consolidation.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:12:51Z"},{"id":2223,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Acceptance verification for 0jm9a.

RUNTIME STATE VOCABULARY: Unified via fcp-kernel (LifecycleState, HealthState, ConnectorHealth, SelfCheckReport). Host consumes kernel types through re-exports. No parallel public definitions.

ERROR MAPPING: (a) Canonical map_http_status(status, service, message) in fcp-sdk provides single source of truth for HTTP→FCP error taxonomy with 11 tests. (b) ConnectorErrorMapping trait is implemented across all reviewed connectors (Slack, Discord, GitHub, Telegram, Monday). (c) Error codes standardized: 401→code 2001, 403→CapabilityDenied, 404→ResourceNotFound, 429→RateLimited, 5xx→External.

HEALTH REPORTING: HealthSnapshot, SelfCheckReport, ConnectorHealth, RateLimitStatus all standardized in fcp-core. Host's parallel HealthState is private (dead code, not exported).

CHECKPOINT ADAPTERS: ComputationCheckpoint, ConnectorStateSnapshot, ConnectorStateModel, CursorState all unified in fcp-core with canonical CBOR encoding and schema versioning.

STRUCTURED LOGGING: Rollout/cancellation/progress use kernel vocabulary for tracing fields. SDK and host emit connector_id, operation, state transitions. Full log schema contract deferred to a dedicated bead.

955 fcp-sdk tests pass. Acceptance criteria met: runtime behavior is explainable in one vocabulary across host, SDK, and connector implementations.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:13:43Z"},{"id":2226,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Claiming a narrow doctor.rs follow-on under 0jm9a. Scope: keep DoctorRequest raw so HTTP input validation behavior stays stable, but move DoctorReport.zone_id plus ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id and derived CheckResult.connector_id onto canonical ZoneId / ConnectorId types. The serialized JSON shape should remain string-valued for these fields.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:20:57Z"},{"id":2231,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","author":"WildTern","text":"Completed doctor typed-identifier follow-up. Kept DoctorRequest.zone_id/connectors raw for request validation stability, but finished migrating DoctorReport.zone_id, ConnectorSelfCheck.connector_id, and CheckResult.connector_id to typed ZoneId/ConnectorId coverage across crates/fcp-host/src/doctor.rs and crates/fcp-host/tests/no_mock_integration.rs. Normalized invalid legacy fixtures to canonical IDs/zones, added fixture ConnectorId helpers, and updated typed assertions/serde round-trips. Verification: rustfmt --edition 2024 passed; git diff --check passed; RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host completed 26-root remote dependency preflight on worker vmi1149989, reached remote Cargo, then failed because the worker is missing nightly-2026-02-15-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu. Interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately when it started waiting on the package-cache lock.","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:30:42Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-0k3d4","title":"[REVIEW-POSTMORTEM][P2] jdaro legacy track() shim leaves untracked-owner ops cross-cancellable","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of commit 8c49b234 (br-jdaro). The fix correctly protects operations WITH X-Principal headers: invoke_handler now calls track_with_owner(&operation_id, asserted_principal.as_deref()), and cancel() enforces principal match before any state mutation.

Residual gap: the legacy track() shim (cancellation.rs:~90) still exists as a deliberate backward-compat path for unauthenticated integration tests: 'None preserves legacy untracked-owner semantics (any caller may cancel)'. The regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded pins this behavior.

Per AGENTS.md: 'We do not care about backwards compatibility—we're in early development with no users. We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT. Never create compatibility shims. Never create wrapper functions for deprecated APIs. Just fix the code directly.' The track() → track_with_owner(id, None) shim is exactly such a compat shim.

Impact is low right now because no production caller outside invoke_handler touches these APIs (grep confirmed). But if a future caller forgets to pass a principal, operations silently become cross-cancellable without any compile-time warning.

Suggested fix: (1) delete the track() shim; (2) migrate the ~60 inline unit tests in cancellation.rs to call track_with_owner(id, None) explicitly (mechanical find-and-replace in the same module); (3) delete the companion regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded OR reframe it as 'unauthenticated path rejects at cancel' to tighten semantics further; (4) consider whether invoke_handler should require X-Principal entirely (reject missing header) rather than silently tracking with None.

Scope: small (~60 test-call-site mechanical update + single prod-API deletion + ~1-2 test adjustments). Improves compile-time safety + aligns with AGENTS.md anti-shim policy.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:23:58.112902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T21:23:58.112902Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0ky","title":"fcp.salesforce: Enterprise SSO integration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:19:42.657324478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T17:09:27.135621851Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T17:09:27.135621851Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: All Salesforce sub-features merged into comprehensive parent connector bead flywheel_connectors-4m0 (fcp.salesforce: Salesforce CRM Platform Connector)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","title":"[FCP3/P5.4] Run a two node pilot with failure drills and evidence capture","description":"Background: the architecture needs a narrow but real proof slice. A two node pilot for one request response family is the smallest credible demonstration that placement, leases, checkpoint handoff, and evidence backed explanation actually work together.

Scope: run a scripted pilot that exercises initial placement, failover, resume, duplicate executor prevention, and operator visible explanation for one connector family across two nodes. Require detailed event timelines, cross node logs, captured control plane evidence, and artifact bundles that make the failure drills reproducible rather than anecdotal.

Acceptance: a real two node scenario works end to end and produces a repeatable, inspectable proof trail with detailed logs and replayable artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.582636Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:55.436164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:55.435941Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-5","pilot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:43.012826Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:41.621408Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:43.711051Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:41.042579Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:42.294358Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2290,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Verification of two-node pilot infrastructure.

PLACEMENT: fcp-mesh PlacementPlanner with deterministic scoring (HRW), device profiles, budget-aware placement. 88 mesh integration tests pass including placement decision paths.

LEASES: LeaseHandoff with monotonic fencing tokens, holder identity binding, checkpoint_object_id linking. Lease expiry enforced. Tested in mesh_integration.rs (LeaseHandoff scenarios at lines 2133, 2330).

CHECKPOINT HANDOFF: Full pipeline tested: suspend→export(Inline|Chunked)→transfer→resume. 232 checkpoint/migration/handoff/resume tests in fcp-core pass. MigratableComputationState FSM: Running→Suspended→Transferring→Running.

FAILOVER: Multi-node failure scenario in conformance (integration_scenarios.rs:71, MULTI_NODE_FAILURE_SCENARIO) exercises crash recovery and authority view failover ordering. Multi-node gossip convergence (mvp_vertical_slice.rs:2055), partition handling (2093), and session-gated symbol transfer (mesh_integration.rs:5907) prove cross-node coordination.

EVIDENCE: CheckpointHandoffArtifact with deterministic ResumeTimelineEvent sequence. ResumeEvidence with freshness assessment, duplicate delivery detection, and resume disposition. All artifacts serializable for replay.

DUPLICATE PREVENTION: Lease fencing tokens prevent split-brain (monotonic token advancement ensures only one active executor).

Tests verified via rch: 88 mesh integration, 232 checkpoint/handoff/resume, 22 conformance multi-node scenarios. Pilot scenarios produce repeatable proof trails with structured logs and artifacts.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0r8rd","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-host tests broken: RevocationFreshnessCheck struct missing (14 compile errors)","description":"crates/fcp-host/src/enforcement.rs:2235-3863 — 10+ test-module call sites reference RevocationFreshnessCheck.name() / .check(), but no struct RevocationFreshnessCheck exists in the crate. grep shows only the test references, plus CheckpointFreshnessCheck and RevocationCheck. Compilation of fcp-host lib tests fails with 14 errors (cannot find value RevocationFreshnessCheck). Also noticed in same test compile: ObjectHeader has no field named metadata (E0560), RevocationObject has no associated schema fn (E0599), EnforcementContextBuilder has no method token_id (E0599). These indicate multiple tests were left behind during a refactor. Repro: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo check --offline -p fcp-host --tests. Suggested fix: either reintroduce a RevocationFreshnessCheck unit struct implementing the policy-check trait, or migrate the tests to the current RevocationCheck + CheckpointFreshnessCheck primitives. Also fix the ObjectHeader.metadata / RevocationObject::schema / EnforcementContextBuilder.token_id references. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:10:00.050628Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:37.502909Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:33:37.502326Z","close_reason":"Repaired revocation enforcement tests; RevocationFreshnessCheck references aligned","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -1175,7 +1176,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","title":"fcp.datadog: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:12:35.809889760Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47.880447Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47.880431Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1128,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Implemented provisioning automation for fcp.datadog — provisioning_recipe() with auth mode selection + secret prompt/store steps, provisioning_readiness() method with auth_mode/network_ok/base_url checks, enhanced handle_self_check() including provisioning info. 16 new tests (202 total unit), all pass, clippy clean. Commit a0ff0b44.","created_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","title":"TEST: fcp.datadog Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T12:12:36.914405664Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:16.513026Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:11.325958Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-h32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":748,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Added 109 tests total (66 unit + 43 integration). Unit: 29 new connector.rs tests (DatadogConfig::from_params with region/credential/validation, require_str/require_i64, operations_info, DoctorResult). Integration: expanded from 35 to 43 with simulate_all_ops, credential_id config, both_auth_rejected, handshake capabilities, missing field validation for logs/metrics/monitors.","created_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:10Z"},{"id":749,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Tests complete: 66 unit tests (types serialization, error retryable/display/fcp-conversion, client region/auth, connector config validation, operations metadata) + 43 integration tests (lifecycle, all 8 operations, error codes 401/403/404/429, missing fields, counters). All pass, clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T08:02:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cu3.4","title":"E2E: fcp.datadog Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cuqvo","title":"[REVIEW R2 P3][MED] fcp-e2e: legacy compliance adapters still fabricate handshake responses","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/docusign_compliance_e2e.rs:82-106 still discards the connector handshake payload via `let _raw = self.connector.handle_handshake(request).await?;` and fabricates `HandshakeResponse` with `SessionId::new()`, static `manifest_hash`, and grants copied from the request. Spot checks found the same residual pattern in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/bigquery_compliance_e2e.rs:68-93 and crates/fcp-e2e/tests/gitlab_compliance_e2e.rs:68-91. Repro: run `for f in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/*.rs; do if rg -q "handle_handshake" "$f" && rg -q "Ok\(HandshakeResponse \{" "$f" && rg -q "SessionId::new\(" "$f" && ! rg -q "missing protocol_version" "$f"; then echo "$f"; fi; done | sort` to list the remaining masked adapters, then inspect any listed handshake body. Fix: apply the spotify/terraform/rz5y1 real-handshake template so each adapter serializes the real `HandshakeRequest`, injects a session id, asserts `protocol_version` / `connector_id` / advertised capabilities from the connector response, and only grants capabilities the connector actually publishes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:30:40.800279Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:37:02.761176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-cuqvo","title":"[REVIEW R2 P3][MED] fcp-e2e: legacy compliance adapters still fabricate handshake responses","description":"crates/fcp-e2e/tests/docusign_compliance_e2e.rs:82-106 still discards the connector handshake payload via `let _raw = self.connector.handle_handshake(request).await?;` and fabricates `HandshakeResponse` with `SessionId::new()`, static `manifest_hash`, and grants copied from the request. Spot checks found the same residual pattern in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/bigquery_compliance_e2e.rs:68-93 and crates/fcp-e2e/tests/gitlab_compliance_e2e.rs:68-91. Repro: run `for f in crates/fcp-e2e/tests/*.rs; do if rg -q "handle_handshake" "$f" && rg -q "Ok\(HandshakeResponse \{" "$f" && rg -q "SessionId::new\(" "$f" && ! rg -q "missing protocol_version" "$f"; then echo "$f"; fi; done | sort` to list the remaining masked adapters, then inspect any listed handshake body. Fix: apply the spotify/terraform/rz5y1 real-handshake template so each adapter serializes the real `HandshakeRequest`, injects a session id, asserts `protocol_version` / `connector_id` / advertised capabilities from the connector response, and only grants capabilities the connector actually publishes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:30:40.800279Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T21:10:44.761627Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T21:10:44.760705Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d1b","title":"fcp.microsoft365: Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector","description":"# FCP Microsoft 365 Suite Integration Connector

## Overview
Comprehensive enterprise connector for Microsoft 365 suite, providing unified access through Microsoft Graph API. Critical for enterprise adoption with support for Outlook, OneDrive, Excel, Word, OneNote, SharePoint, and MS Planner.

## Sub-Connectors Architecture

### 1. Outlook (Email & Calendar)

#### Email Operations
- **Read Messages**: Inbox, folders, search
- **Send Messages**: Compose, reply, forward
- **Manage Messages**: Move, delete, archive
- **Attachments**: Upload, download, inline
- **Mail Folders**: Create, organize, rules

#### Calendar Operations
- **Events**: Create, update, delete, RSVP
- **Calendars**: Multiple calendar support
- **Availability**: Free/busy lookup
- **Scheduling**: Find meeting times
- **Recurring Events**: Complex patterns

#### Contacts
- **Contact Management**: CRUD operations
- **Contact Folders**: Organization
- **Contact Groups**: Distribution lists

### 2. OneDrive (File Storage)

#### File Operations
- **Upload**: Simple and resumable
- **Download**: Direct and streaming
- **Copy/Move**: Within and across drives
- **Delete**: Soft delete and restore
- **Sharing**: Links, permissions

#### Folder Management
- **Create/Delete Folders**
- **List Contents**: Pagination support
- **Search**: Full-text search
- **Delta Sync**: Track changes

#### Special Folders
- Documents, Pictures, Desktop
- App-specific folders
- Shared folders

### 3. Excel Online

#### Workbook Operations
- **Open/Create Workbooks**
- **List Worksheets**
- **Workbook Sessions**: Persistent editing

#### Data Operations
```python
# Read range
GET /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10')

# Update range
PATCH /workbook/worksheets/{id}/range(address='A1:D10')
{
    "values": [["A", "B"], ["C", "D"]]
}
```

#### Table Operations
- **Create Tables**: From ranges
- **Add Rows**: Append data
- **Sort/Filter**: Data manipulation
- **Calculated Columns**: Formulas

#### Charts & Pivots
- Create charts from data
- Pivot table operations

### 4. Word Online

#### Document Operations
- **Create Documents**: From templates
- **Read Content**: Full text extraction
- **Update Content**: Append, replace
- **Export**: PDF, other formats

#### Content Controls
- Find and replace
- Bookmark management
- Comment operations

### 5. OneNote

#### Notebook Operations
- **List Notebooks**: Personal and shared
- **Create Notebooks**
- **Sections**: Create, organize
- **Section Groups**: Nested organization

#### Page Operations
```python
# Create page with HTML content
POST /sections/{id}/pages
Content-Type: text/html

<html>
  <head><title>Page Title</title></head>
  <body>
    <h1>Heading</h1>
    <p>Content here</p>
  </body>
</html>
```

#### Content
- Rich text with HTML
- Images and attachments
- Ink and handwriting
- Tags and to-do items

### 6. SharePoint

#### Site Operations
- **List Sites**: All accessible sites
- **Site Details**: Properties, permissions
- **Subsites**: Create, manage

#### Lists & Libraries
- **Document Libraries**: File storage
- **Custom Lists**: Structured data
- **List Items**: CRUD operations
- **Views**: Custom views

#### Pages & News
- **Site Pages**: Create, update
- **News Posts**: Publishing
- **Web Parts**: Content blocks

### 7. MS Planner

#### Plan Operations
- **Create Plans**: Within Groups
- **List Plans**: User's plans
- **Plan Details**: Buckets, tasks

#### Task Operations
```python
{
    "planId": "...",
    "bucketId": "...",
    "title": "Task title",
    "assignments": {
        "user-id": {
            "@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.plannerAssignment",
            "orderHint": " !"
        }
    },
    "dueDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z"
}
```

#### Bucket Management
- Create/organize buckets
- Move tasks between buckets
- Bucket ordering

## Microsoft Graph API (Unified Endpoint)

### Base Configuration
```yaml
graph_api:
  base_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0"
  beta_url: "https://graph.microsoft.com/beta"
  scopes:
    - "User.Read"
    - "Mail.ReadWrite"
    - "Files.ReadWrite.All"
    - "Calendars.ReadWrite"
    - "Sites.ReadWrite.All"
    - "Tasks.ReadWrite"
```

### Batch Requests
```python
POST /$batch
{
    "requests": [
        {"id": "1", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/messages"},
        {"id": "2", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/events"},
        {"id": "3", "method": "GET", "url": "/me/drive/root/children"}
    ]
}
```

## OAuth 2.0 with Refresh Tokens

### Authorization Flow
```
1. GET /authorize?client_id=...&scope=...&redirect_uri=...
2. User consents
3. POST /token (authorization_code grant)
4. Store refresh_token securely
5. POST /token (refresh_token grant) when access_token expires
```

### Token Management
```yaml
auth:
  type: "oauth2"
  client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}"
  client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
  tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}"
  redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback"
  
  token_storage:
    type: "encrypted_file"
    path: "/secure/tokens.enc"
    
  auto_refresh: true
  refresh_buffer_seconds: 300
```

### Scopes by Sub-connector
| Sub-connector | Required Scopes |
|---------------|-----------------|
| Outlook Mail | Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, Mail.Send |
| Outlook Calendar | Calendars.Read, Calendars.ReadWrite |
| OneDrive | Files.Read, Files.ReadWrite, Files.ReadWrite.All |
| Excel | Files.ReadWrite |
| Word | Files.ReadWrite |
| OneNote | Notes.Read, Notes.ReadWrite |
| SharePoint | Sites.Read.All, Sites.ReadWrite.All |
| Planner | Tasks.Read, Tasks.ReadWrite, Group.ReadWrite.All |

## Webhook Subscriptions (Real-time Events)

### Subscription Management
```python
# Create subscription
POST /subscriptions
{
    "changeType": "created,updated,deleted",
    "notificationUrl": "https://webhook.example.com/m365",
    "resource": "/me/messages",
    "expirationDateTime": "2024-12-31T00:00:00Z",
    "clientState": "secret-validation-token"
}
```

### Supported Resources
- `/me/messages`: Email changes
- `/me/events`: Calendar changes
- `/me/contacts`: Contact changes
- `/drives/{id}/root`: OneDrive changes
- `/sites/{id}/lists/{id}/items`: SharePoint changes
- `/groups/{id}/planner/tasks`: Planner changes

### Webhook Handler
```python
async def handle_webhook(request):
    # Validation challenge
    if 'validationToken' in request.query:
        return PlainTextResponse(request.query['validationToken'])
    
    # Process notifications
    for notification in request.json()['value']:
        resource = notification['resource']
        change_type = notification['changeType']
        # Process change...
```

### Subscription Renewal
- Max lifetime: 3 days (most resources)
- Auto-renewal before expiration
- Webhook validation handling

## Configuration Schema
```yaml
fcp.microsoft365:
  tenant_id: "${AZURE_TENANT_ID}"
  client_id: "${AZURE_CLIENT_ID}"
  client_secret: "${AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET}"
  
  auth:
    type: "oauth2"
    redirect_uri: "http://localhost:8080/callback"
    token_cache: "/secure/token_cache.json"
    
  sub_connectors:
    outlook:
      enabled: true
      mail_folders: ["Inbox", "Sent Items"]
      calendar_sync: true
      
    onedrive:
      enabled: true
      root_path: "/Documents"
      
    excel:
      enabled: true
      
    word:
      enabled: true
      
    onenote:
      enabled: true
      
    sharepoint:
      enabled: true
      sites: ["https://tenant.sharepoint.com/sites/team"]
      
    planner:
      enabled: true
      
  webhooks:
    enabled: true
    endpoint_url: "https://webhook.example.com/m365"
    resources:
      - "/me/messages"
      - "/me/events"
      
  rate_limits:
    requests_per_second: 10
    concurrent_connections: 5
```

## MCP Tool Definitions

### Outlook Tools
- `outlook_list_messages`: List emails
- `outlook_send_message`: Send email
- `outlook_create_event`: Create calendar event
- `outlook_get_availability`: Check free/busy

### OneDrive Tools
- `onedrive_list_files`: List directory contents
- `onedrive_upload_file`: Upload file
- `onedrive_download_file`: Download file
- `onedrive_share_file`: Create sharing link

### Excel Tools
- `excel_read_range`: Read cell range
- `excel_write_range`: Write to cells
- `excel_create_table`: Create data table
- `excel_run_function`: Execute formula

### SharePoint Tools
- `sharepoint_list_sites`: List sites
- `sharepoint_get_list_items`: Read list data
- `sharepoint_create_list_item`: Add list item

### Planner Tools
- `planner_list_tasks`: List user tasks
- `planner_create_task`: Create new task
- `planner_update_task`: Update task status

### Webhook Tools
- `m365_create_subscription`: Create webhook
- `m365_list_subscriptions`: List active webhooks
- `m365_delete_subscription`: Remove webhook

## Enterprise Features

### Multi-tenant Support
- Per-tenant configuration
- Tenant isolation
- Cross-tenant operations (with consent)

### Compliance & Governance
- Audit logging
- Data residency awareness
- eDiscovery integration

### Delegated vs Application Permissions
- User context operations
- Background service operations
- Admin consent flows

## Error Handling
- Token refresh failures
- Rate limit (429) handling
- Throttling with Retry-After
- Permission denied errors
- Resource not found

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Core Auth (Week 1-2)
- [ ] OAuth 2.0 flow implementation
- [ ] Token storage and refresh
- [ ] Multi-tenant support
- [ ] Permission management

### Phase 2: Outlook (Week 3-4)
- [ ] Mail operations
- [ ] Calendar operations
- [ ] Contact operations

### Phase 3: OneDrive & Files (Week 5-6)
- [ ] File operations
- [ ] Excel integration
- [ ] Word integration

### Phase 4: Collaboration (Week 7-8)
- [ ] SharePoint integration
- [ ] OneNote integration
- [ ] Planner integration

### Phase 5: Real-time (Week 9-10)
- [ ] Webhook subscriptions
- [ ] Change notifications
- [ ] Delta sync

### Phase 6: Enterprise (Week 11-12)
- [ ] Compliance features
- [ ] Monitoring
- [ ] Documentation

## Dependencies
- msal (Microsoft Authentication Library)
- httpx (async HTTP client)
- cryptography (token encryption)
- pydantic (data validation)

## Success Metrics
- OAuth flow success rate > 99%
- API call latency < 500ms
- Webhook delivery success > 99.9%
- Support all listed sub-connectors

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.
